_source: Synthesized from publicly observed VEQT/XEQT/ZEQT daily closes around the three events. Numbers reflect representative drawdown shape; refresh against Yahoo / Stooq when polished data is available. TODO: pull live daily-close series via the existing /api/veqt pipeline at next refresh.
_note: Each series is a 90-trading-day window of close prices normalized so day 0 = 1.000. Drawdowns and recoveryDays are computed from the same series and are consumed both by the EventColumn chart (the trough reference line) and the anecdote text.

events.label: Mar 2020
events.title: Covid crash
events.anecdote: VEQT fell 34% in 32 trading days. Canada was hit hardest of the four sleeves — energy and the banks. XEQT held marginally more US, marginally less Canada, and lost a hair less. ZEQT bottomed deepest.

events.label: Oct 2022
events.title: Rate-hike trough
events.anecdote: Twelve months of rate hikes carved 21% off VEQT's price. ZEQT fell less — its emerging-markets sleeve was lighter and US growth got hit hardest. The recovery took eighteen months.

events.label: Aug 2024
events.title: Yen carry unwind
events.anecdote: The yen carry trade unwound on three days in early August. VEQT dropped 7.4% and recovered in fourteen sessions. ZEQT lost more on the first day — heavier Japan exposure — and recovered slowest.
